title: Live Well, Feel Strong
objectives: Outline a two-paragraph report plan about healthy habits and sports benefits, using topic sentences, details, and linkers
Produce a clear expository report about healthy living, using appropriate vocabulary and cohesive devices

key_concepts.explanation: A report has four parts: Introduction (opens the topic, must have a general topic sentence), Paragraph 1 (specific topic sentence about the body — sleep, food, water — with supporting details and a concluding idea), Paragraph 2 (specific topic sentence about the mind — helping, staying calm — with supporting details and a concluding idea), Conclusion (summary of paragraph 1 and paragraph 2 main ideas).

Each body paragraph starts with one main idea (topic sentence), adds details or examples, and ends with a short concluding thought. Example introduction: 'A healthy life helps people feel happy.' Example conclusion: 'In conclusion, healthy habits and sports keep our bodies and minds strong.'
Sleeping early and eating well help the body stay strong. Drinking enough water keeps us active and ready to learn.
Helping others and staying calm make life happier. Smiling, listening, and speaking kindly build good relationships and reduce stress.

exercises.problem: Match each healthy habit/sport to what it improves: a. Eating fruits and vegetables → Mind, b. Spending time with family → Teamwork, c. Playing football → Relationships, d. Mental sports → Energy
exercises.answer: a → Energy (body nourishment), b → Relationships, c → Teamwork, d → Mind

exercises.problem: Read Paragraph A (Healthy Body) and Paragraph B (Healthy Mind). Answer: What do these two paragraphs talk about? Write one sentence that can introduce both paragraphs. Write one sentence that can summarise both paragraphs.
exercises.answer: Both talk about ways to stay healthy in body and mind. Introduction: A healthy life helps people feel happy. Summary: In conclusion, healthy habits and sports keep our bodies and minds strong.

exercises.problem: Fill in the table with your own sentence starters for each part: Introduction (opens topic), Paragraph 1 (body — sleep, food, water), Paragraph 2 (mind — helping, staying calm), Conclusion (summary).
Write a full two-paragraph report about healthy living. Use the report structure: introduction, paragraph 1 (healthy body), paragraph 2 (healthy mind), conclusion.

teaching_notes: Writing lesson teaching two-paragraph expository report structure. Scaffolded approach: Match and Talk warm-up → read model paragraphs → plan with table → write full report. Writing Tip: Each body paragraph starts with one main idea, adds details or examples, and ends with a short concluding thought. Introduction starts the report, conclusion ends it. Page 28 contains the planning table and final draft space. 2 pages.
